title: veronica officinalis var. carquejana
intro: Veronica officinalis var. carquejana, also known as Carqueja, is a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Carqueja include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Carqueja can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support digestive health and reduce fever.
